hermes.goal: Beat buy-and-hold (positive alpha vs BTC) while keeping max drawdown under 15% and never risking the mandate on a single trade — capital preservation first. Near-term: prove a sustained, fee-aware, positive out-of-sample edge on the paper trainer over ~90 days before scaling any live capital; grow the live account only on demonstrated alpha.

brain.commentary: All four council verdicts are flat with uniformly negative composites (-0.15 to -0.36), zero-to-negative alpha across the board, and elevated realized vols (0.39-0.55). ETH and SOL carry beta ~1.25, so they add correlated downside rather than diversification. With no positive edge anywhere in the book, capital preservation dominates: I am setting a risk-off posture and scaling gross exposure to 0.6x.
